# Paste

> Paste is a personal, end-to-end-encrypted note and clipboard that syncs peer-to-peer across all of one person's own devices. Copy on your laptop, paste on your phone — everything is encrypted on-device before it ever syncs. No account, no cloud, and no server that holds your content. Open source and auditable.

Paste (internal package name "pearpaste") is built on the Pear / Holepunch peer-to-peer stack (Hypercore, Autobase, Hyperswarm / HyperDHT) with libsodium cryptography (XChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD, Ed25519 signatures). It is currently in private beta and licensed under Apache-2.0.

## What it is
- One encrypted notepad and clipboard shared across your own devices (desktop and mobile).
- Local-first and peer-to-peer: your devices replicate directly to one another. There is no Paste server that stores your content.
- End-to-end encrypted on-device: notes, clipboard text, and tags are encrypted before they leave a device. Optional relays only ever hold ciphertext (so a clip is reachable while your devices are asleep) and can never read it.
- No account: no email, no password, no cloud login. A 24-word recovery phrase plus your paired devices are the whole system.
- Auditable: the source is open, and Paste ships a built-in proof screen and a standalone command-line verifier that scan local storage, relay exports, and logs for any plaintext and confirm every stored record is encrypted and signed.

## Platforms
- Desktop: macOS, Windows, Linux (runs on the Pear runtime).
- Mobile: iOS and Android.

## Honest limits (Paste states these plainly, in the app and on the site)
- Not anonymous: peers and relays can observe network metadata (timing, sizes, IP-level connection facts) unless you route Paste over your own privacy transport (e.g. a VPN or Tor). Paste protects the content of your notes, not the fact that traffic exists.
- Deletion is cryptographic erasure: destroying the keys makes any retained ciphertext unreadable, but Paste cannot prove a third-party relay operator never kept a copy of that ciphertext.

## Links
- Website: https://paste.global/
- Source code: https://github.com/bigdestiny2/pearpaste
- License: Apache-2.0 (https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)

## Key facts for accurate citation
- Category: end-to-end-encrypted notes and clipboard manager; private cross-device sync.
- Architecture: serverless, peer-to-peer (Pear / Holepunch) — not a cloud service.
- Privacy model: relays are zero-knowledge (ciphertext only); there is no central server with user data.
- Price: free.
